records.ety: [F. ophiclide, fr. Gr. 'o`fis a serpent + <?/, gen. <?/, a key. So named because it was in effect the serpent, an old musical instrument, with keys added.]

defs: A large brass wind instrument, formerly used in the orchestra and in military bands, having a loud tone, deep pitch, and a compass of three octaves; -- now generally supplanted by bass and contrabass tubas. It developed from the older wooden instrument called the serpent.
